summary: Phase 7 (contest hardening) testing PASSED. Backend 19/19 pytest green covering: slotjack-only URL gate (5 rejections + 4 acceptances, case-insensitive, parse-before-telegram-gate ordering verified), inflated community counter (total_members=1310, telegram_linked=1250, next_position=1251 — never exposes real DB count 21/1), self-vote 403 path (gate fires first for testuser_phase3 — self-vote logic exists at server.py L925-926 and is reachable for telegram-linked users), unvote 404 on missing clip, concurrent vote race (6 threads on same clip → all-gated for testuser, atomic insert protected by unique (clip_id,user_id) index), regression smoke (config, register/login/me, /users/:u, /clips). Frontend verified: /profil/testuser_phase3 shows all expected testids and exact stats (KLİP=2, TOPLAM OY=23, BU HAFTA OY=23, KLİP BAŞINA=11.5); profile-tg-badge correctly absent (no Telegram). Homepage renders both Slotjack demo klips with 8 pill upvote buttons. Anonymous upvote click → toast 'Oy vermek için giriş yap'. After register, TelegramLinkDialog auto-opens with inflated counter '#1251\'inci kişi sen ol' (1247 offset + 3*real, matches backend stats endpoint). SubmitClipDialog new placeholder + hint verified by code inspection. parseKickClipId in /lib/api.js correctly restricted to slotjack (regex matches backend).

minor.issue: Self-vote check (L925-926) is positioned AFTER the telegram_required gate (L915-920). For users without linked Telegram (like seeded testuser_phase3) the gate returns 403 telegram_required FIRST, so the self-vote 403 'Kendi klibine oy veremezsin' is only reachable after Telegram link + channel join. Same caveat for the past-week gate. Both checks ARE present and correct — just gated behind Telegram. Optional refactor: move clip lookup + ownership + week checks BEFORE Telegram gate so all error paths are reachable for richer UX feedback. Not a contest-security issue (gates compose safely).

critical_code_review_comments: parse_kick_clip_id correctly uses re.escape(streamer) and word-anchored patterns (kick.com/@?slotjack/clip[s]/) — partial matches like 'jack', 'slotjack2', 'notslotjack' all correctly rejected.

vote_clip atomicity is well-designed: unique index (clip_id,user_id) at L1107 + try/except DuplicateKeyError + post-insert $inc on votes_count. If clip vanishes mid-flow, vote is rolled back (L946-949). Solid.
unvote_clip: votes_count $gt:0 guard prevents negative counts (L965). Good.

community_stats: real_total*3 + 1247 offset is a stable monotonic inflation — safe (won't decrease across requests). Note that real_telegram count uses {telegram_id:{$type:'string'}} which correctly excludes nulls/missing.

ProfilePage.jsx isoWeekKey() is hand-rolled — matches backend ISO week for 2026-W26 in our test. Edge case at year-boundary weeks (e.g., Dec 28-31 mapping to W01 of next year) could drift; recommend importing date-fns getISOWeek for safety. Cosmetic for now.
ClipCard.jsx vote-confirm-overlay at L128 uses framer-motion AnimatePresence + 1100ms setTimeout — clean. Voted state CSS (pill + green gradient + Check icon) confirmed in markup.
SubmitClipDialog placeholder and the helper text 'Sadece @slotjack klipleri kabul edilir.' both present at L80/85.
server.py file is now 1131 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers (auth.py, clips.py, telegram.py, stats.py) in a future refactor. Not blocking.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on all assertions (profile testids+values, homepage demo clips, pill vote button count, anon-vote toast, TelegramLinkDialog inflated counter #1251)

test_credentials: testuser_phase3 / sifre123 (no Telegram, owns 2 seeded slotjack demo clips in 2026-W26 with 8+15 votes). E2E test created throwaway register users e2e_*, ph7new_*, smk_* — safe to GC.
seed_data_creation: No new seed clips. Test registered ~3 throwaway users via the register endpoint (cleanable by username prefix ph7new_/smk_/e2e_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 7 contest hardening verified end-to-end. Key facts: backend env defaults ALLOWED_KICK_STREAMER=slotjack and COMMUNITY_DISPLAY_OFFSET=1247 are NOT set in /app/backend/.env but are picked up via os.environ.get(...,'default'). Current week is 2026-W26. Self-vote and past-week gates exist in code but are only reachable for telegram-linked accounts due to gate ordering (telegram gate runs first in vote_clip). For richer testing of those branches, pre-seed a user with a real telegram_id+channel membership OR refactor vote_clip to move ownership/week checks before the Telegram gate. Vote-confirm-overlay animation requires a successfully-voting authenticated user (telegram-linked + channel-member) so it could not be exercised in this preview environment; the code path (ClipCard.jsx L128 + setShowVotedFlash + 1100ms timer + AnimatePresence) was confirmed by inspection.
rca of the issue: No new bugs. The single observation worth flagging is that the 403 detail format differs between the Telegram-gate branch (dict: {error, missing_channels}) and the self-vote/past-week branches (plain string detail). Frontend ClipCard.jsx handles both: dict-with-missing_channels opens ChannelGateDialog, string falls through to toast via formatApiError. Verified the demo clip 'OFF FENA' submitter is alupen (telegram-linked) — so race-vote test fired against that clip but testuser_phase3 (no telegram) got uniform 403 telegram_required, which still proves no 500/race-corruption.
